main.description: A bud: one contribution to a story tree. Freshly written buds grow for 24h (not yet followable); after that they open as 'blooms' that other writers can extend; past 48h without children they lock into 'branches'. These lifecycle stages are views of the same record, not different record types. The AppView enforces a 500-word ceiling on `text` (counted via Unicode word segmentation); writes that exceed it are rejected as WordLimitExceeded. A bud is either a continuation (`parent` set) or a root (`parent` unset). Root buds authored by a trusted seed author are accepted as-is; other root buds must carry `grant` as a strong ref to an ink.branchline.seedGrant authorizing this author to plant a seed. `parent` and `grant` are mutually exclusive.

parent.description: Strong ref to the parent bud being continued. Mutually exclusive with `grant`. Absent on roots.

grant.description: Strong ref to the ink.branchline.seedGrant this bud was planted from. Mutually exclusive with `parent`. Only valid on root buds whose author matches the grant's terminal `recipient`; the AppView walks the grant chain back to a trusted grantor and rejects the bud if any hop fails.

start.description: Byte offset (inclusive) into `text` where the span begins.

end.description: Byte offset (exclusive) into `text` where the span ends.
